V-M-063.purpose: Verify Phase-45 four-day trial and first-VPN-handshake activation semantics across billing, VPN, scheduler, and user cabinet UX.
V-M-063.checks: Pending trial is created after email verification, first config remains available, first successful VPN handshake activates the four-day countdown exactly once, subscription API returns precise remaining time, and mobile UI shows countdown plus calendar without stale 3-day copy.

phase-45-gates.gate-1: Verified registration creates a pending four-day trial that has no running countdown before first VPN handshake.
phase-45-gates.gate-2: Pending trial permits first VPN config retrieval and successful VPN connection path.
phase-45-gates.gate-3: First VPN handshake sets started_at and expires_at once; repeated handshakes do not extend trial.
phase-45-gates.gate-4: Paid, complimentary, expired, and already-active subscriptions are not overwritten by pending-trial activation.
phase-45-gates.gate-5: Subscription API exposes remaining seconds/days/hours/minutes and active date range from server time.
phase-45-gates.gate-6: Dashboard and subscription pages show pending activation, countdown, and calendar without horizontal mobile scroll.

phase-53-ui-gates.gate-1: Matrix dashboard and subscription redesign keep pending-first-VPN state explicit and do not imply the countdown has started before the first successful VPN handshake.
phase-53-ui-gates.gate-2: Activated subscriptions show server-derived days, hours, minutes, start date, expiry date, and compact active-date calendar without text overlap.
phase-53-ui-gates.gate-3: Expired, paid, complimentary, and pending states remain visually distinct and do not reuse stale 3-day trial copy.
phase-53-ui-gates.gate-4: Phase-53 frontend changes do not alter billing, VPN, scheduler, migration, or API response semantics.

verification-levels.module-local: Billing pending-trial state, activation idempotency, VPN pending access, scheduler activation, and subscription countdown math are covered with deterministic backend assertions.
verification-levels.wave-level: Verified-registration onboarding, VPN config access, scheduler stats refresh, billing state, and frontend subscription rendering are verified as one behavior wave.
verification-levels.phase-level: Backend regression suite, frontend build, subscription UX smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and no deploy/install diff guard must all pass before Phase-45 is closed.

scenarios.scenario-1: User verifies email, receives pending trial, downloads config, and UI says timer starts after first VPN connection.
scenarios.scenario-2: First handshake activates exactly four days of trial from handshake timestamp.
scenarios.scenario-3: Second handshake does not move expires_at forward.
scenarios.scenario-4: Paid user handshake does not convert or shorten paid subscription.
scenarios.scenario-5: Expired pending or ineligible trial does not create unauthorized VPN access.
scenarios.scenario-6: Matrix subscription UI preserves pending/countdown/calendar semantics on mobile and desktop viewports.
scenarios.scenario-7: Visual redesign hides pending activation guidance, active countdown, or calendar dates behind decorative sections.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_trial_activation.py tests/test_billing_service.py tests/test_verified_registration.py tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py tests/test_scheduler.py tests/test_db_migrations.py -v

python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-45.xml docs/modules/M-063.xml docs/verification/V-M-063.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml docs/requirements.xml docs/current-status.xml

telemetry-requirements.metric-1: Pending trial created, activated, skipped_already_active, skipped_ineligible, and activation lag outcomes are counted without logging VPN keys or client config.
telemetry-requirements.metric-2: Countdown responses expose server-derived remaining time only; no frontend clock is trusted for billing state.

redaction.item-1: No VPN private key, config text, QR payload, access token, or raw environment value may appear in activation logs or smoke output.
redaction.item-2: Handshake activation logs may include user/client ids and timestamps but must not include client config material.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-06-01: Backend Phase-45 regression suite passed outside sandbox due known aiosqlite sandbox hang: 35 passed.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-06-01: cd frontend && npm run build passed.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-06-01: node scripts/phase45-trial-subscription-ux-smoke.mjs passed and confirmed pending/countdown/calendar wiring with no stale 3-day copy.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-06-01: py_compile passed for changed backend runtime and test files; deploy/install diff guard showed no changes.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-06-01: XML parse and MyGRACE lint passed with zero errors and zero warnings after documentation synchronization.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-06-03: Phase-53 frontend build and static smoke passed for Matrix countdown/calendar readability, pending-first-VPN state, no stale 3-day copy, and protected deploy/install isolation.
evidence.evidence-7: 2026-06-04: Phase-68 shared SubscriptionPanel smoke passed for the exact first-connection copy, server-derived countdown fields, compact calendar, and removal of the extra pending warning badge.

V-M-063.autonomous-execution-safety: Conditionally safe for local autonomous execute after Phase-44 or in a separate approved wave. Block execution if pending trial cannot retrieve first VPN config, if activation can extend paid/active subscriptions, or if migration coverage is missing.

phase-68-verification-design.scope: Subscription UX verification after moving the subscription section to /dashboard.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-1: Pending trial copy must say the 4-day timer starts after first connection, not after registration or "handshake".
phase-68-verification-design.gate-2: Warning badge "Trial начнется после первого VPN подключения" and phase57 compact detail block must be absent.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-3: Remaining days/hours/minutes and compact subscription calendar must be server-derived and must not restart locally.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-4: Tariff selection must preserve backend plan IDs, prices, device limits, and checkout contract.
phase-68-verification-design.commands: backend trial tests; cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase45-trial-subscription-ux-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs

phase-69-verification-design.gate-1: If referrer has an active subscription, a paid referral must extend the existing subscription by 7 days exactly once.
phase-69-verification-design.gate-2: If referrer has no active subscription, the 7-day referral reward must be stored as pending access and start only on referrer's first VPN connection.
phase-69-verification-design.gate-3: Multiple webhook retries or payment replays must not duplicate referral bonus days.
phase-69-verification-design.commands: backend referral, billing, trial activation, scheduler, and migration tests; node scripts/phase69-referral-bonus-smoke.mjs

V-M-063.phase-69-evidence: 2026-06-04: tests/test_trial_activation.py and tests/test_scheduler.py passed for pending referral-bonus activation from first VPN handshake and scheduler scan; frontend build and Phase-69 smoke passed for truthful pending bonus copy.

phase-71-verification-design.scope: Subscription copy and calendar boundary marker verification.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-1: The user-visible backend-time explanatory sentence must be absent while countdown fields remain server-derived.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-2: Calendar rangeStart and rangeEnd days must have distinct pulsing/flicker cyberpunk boundary classes with reduced-motion fallback.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-3: Pending first-VPN activation, active countdown, and cross-month calendar behavior from Phase-45/68 must remain intact.
phase-71-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase45-trial-subscription-ux-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs

V-M-063.phase-71-evidence: Backend trial activation tests passed within the 31-test backend suite; frontend build and Phase-71 smoke passed for backend-time copy removal and rangeStart/rangeEnd subscription calendar boundary markers.
